column6: Mythological island home of the enchantress Circe in Homer's Odyssey
Mythological realm of the afterlife reserved for the virtuous and heroic in Greek mythology, described as a paradise or blessed land
Mythological abyss or prison in the underworld where the Titans and other condemned beings are imprisoned and punished in Greek mythology
Mythological realm of the dead in Greek mythology, ruled by Hades and inhabited by various spirits and entities
In Greek mythology, Mount Olympus is the legendary home of the twelve Olympian gods, located in northern Greece
Mythological section of the underworld where the souls of ordinary or indifferent individuals reside in Greek mythology
Mythological section of the underworld where the souls of the wicked are punished in Greek mythology
Mythological river in the underworld, serving as a boundary between the mortal world and the underworld in Greek mythology
Mythological river in the underworld, associated with the River of Pain or woe in Greek mythology
Mythological river in the underworld, associated with the River of Lamentation in Greek mythology
Mythological river in the underworld, associated with oblivion or forgetfulness in Greek mythology
Mythological island where the nymph Calypso resides, mentioned in Homer's Odyssey
The Labyrinth is a mythological maze created by Daedalus on the island of Crete to contain the Minotaur
Mount Othrys is a principal mountain associated with the Titans in Greek mythology, serving as their stronghold during the Titanomachy
